page: Amazon Web Services c5n.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 42.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.62 GB of memory per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX and AVX-512F, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data places the c5n.4xlarge in the average performance category across most metrics. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1170.0 and a multi-core score of 7317.0. In stress-ng tests, single-core performance registers at 1407.28 and multi-core at 14808.85. The memory subsystem delivers a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 826.07 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 45.22 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance processes Redis SET operations at 13.96 million operations per second and handles static web serving throughput at 11.58 GB/sec.

A key tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached EBS volumes, though the instance is network-optimized with a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Additionally, while the instance handles smaller language models adequately, delivering 741.84 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M model, performance drops to a weak rating of 24.09 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model, and it lacks the capacity for 70B models. This hardware profile is suited for compute-intensive workloads, network-bound applications, software compilation, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8124M processor. It provides 16 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 42.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on its 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and network operations. Benchmark results show average tier performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7317.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 15733.02. It processes Redis SET operations at 13.96 million operations per second and delivers 11.58 GB/sec in static web serving. While efficient for compute-bound tasks, software compilation, and network-heavy applications, it exhibits weak performance on medium-sized LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 42.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, suitable for network-bound and compute workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR4 memory, and optimized network bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.

42.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance across CPU, memory, and database benchmarks
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and network-bound tasks
